message:
feat(glibc_internal): port __libc_alloca_cutoff / __libc_use_alloca (bd-oh49d)

Adds the glibc internal alloca-safety helpers consulted by NSS
modules, locale parsers, sprintf width handling, and other
internal callers when deciding whether `size` bytes can safely
be allocated on the current thread's stack via alloca() or
whether they must fall back to malloc.

Both `__libc_alloca_cutoff(size)` and `__libc_use_alloca(size)`
return 1 if size is below the safety threshold, 0 otherwise. The
threshold matches glibc's convention:
`min(PTHREAD_STACK_MIN/4, 65536)`. Computed as a `const` from
libc::PTHREAD_STACK_MIN so the value tracks the host's
PTHREAD_STACK_MIN automatically.

Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers
returning the bit-test result. `__libc_use_alloca` delegates to
`__libc_alloca_cutoff`; glibc exports both names because some
callers reference one and some the other.

Tests: 3 abi integration tests covering: small sizes (0/1/64/1K)
return 1; huge sizes (1MB/1GB/SIZE_MAX) return 0; the two
entrypoints agree across a fixed sweep of representative sizes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-e2ct","title":"syscall: close_range invalid flags on reversed bounds should still return EINVAL","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for close_range(5, 4, 0x80000000). Add ABI regression coverage so bad flags plus reversed bounds stay in the exact EINVAL bucket.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:32:32.686596433Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T00:43:27.336651943Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T00:43:27.334571755Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-e3y8","title":"wordexp parity: quote-aware badchar and command-substitution semantics","description":"Background:
- `wordexp` is marked Implemented, but current parsing is too naive about quoting/escaping context.
- Host glibc accepts quoted and escaped metacharacters literally and only rejects true unquoted shell separators.

Goal:
- Bring the native `wordexp` implementation into parity with host glibc for a bounded shell-lexing slice.

Deliverables:
1) Quote-aware/escape-aware bad-character validation for `wordexp`.
2) Quote-aware command-substitution detection so `WRDE_NOCMD` only rejects real command substitution.
3) Host-parity ABI tests for quoted semicolons, escaped separators, quoted command substitution literals, and bare bad-char cases.

Verification:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test unistd_abi_test wordexp_ -- --nocapture
- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets
- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:01:59.732205136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:09:46.852185122Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:09:46.850227737Z","close_reason":"Match glibc wordexp quote/escape badchar and WRDE_NOCMD semantics with host-parity tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-e4h4","title":"resolv: extend async-DNS ABI tests with explicit GNU mode constants and badflags expectations","description":"The current unistd_abi async-DNS tests exercise degenerate success cases but not GNU mode semantics. Add explicit GAI_WAIT/GAI_NOWAIT/badflags constants in the ABI test layer and verify errno/output contracts for both valid and invalid mode combinations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:39:25.697508593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:14:17.906979579Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:14:17.904743048Z","close_reason":"Extended async-DNS ABI tests with explicit GNU mode and badflags constants and verified the invalid-mode errno contract against host libc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-eabvx","title":"PORT: bsearch_r (NetBSD reentrant bsearch with thunk)","description":"Port the NetBSD `void *bsearch_r(const void *key, const void *base, size_t nmemb, size_t size, int (*compar)(const void *, const void *, void *), void *thunk)` reentrant binary search. Same as POSIX bsearch but the comparator receives an extra `void *thunk` parameter so callers can carry context (e.g. case-insensitive flag, sort key index) without a global. Implementation: thin abi shim implementing standard binary search via the existing memory layout helpers; compar invoked with (key, mid_elem, thunk). NULL key/base/compar returns NULL; size==0 or nmemb==0 returns NULL. Tests cover found / not-found, leftmost/rightmost positions, NULL guards, thunk passes through unchanged.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:58:39.348039681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:58:39.348039681Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-ef2","title":"I/O migration wave: route core unistd/io ABI entrypoints through raw veneer","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Deliverables:
- Migrate open/close/read/write/pread64/pwrite64/lseek/fcntl subset used by smoke suite.
- Preserve strict mode ABI semantics and hardened mode evidence emission.

Acceptance:
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite passes with migration enabled.
- Compatibility fixtures show no semantic drift on return values/errno.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.198369909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:20:06.228805Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:20:06.228805Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","io","syscall"]}

 {"id":"bd-oai.6","title":"Dual-Mode: Logging - mode selection, snapshot events, evidence emission","description":"Logging spec for dual-mode: TRACE for per-call mode dispatch decision, DEBUG for snapshot capture timing and field population, INFO for mode selection at startup and evidence record emission, WARN for snapshot field out-of-range values, ERROR for mode violation attempts (e.g., mode switch after startup). All records include trace_id+decision_id.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:42.187969197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T06:09:45.353113266Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T06:09:45.353015473Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dual-mode","frankenlibc","logging","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oai.6","depends_on_id":"bd-oai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1131,"issue_id":"bd-oai.6","author":"MagentaFalcon","text":"2026-02-25 completion pass (MagentaFalcon): implemented dual-mode logging spec across runtime policy + runtime math export paths. In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs, added bounded JSONL mode-event log with trace_id+decision_id and events for startup selection (info), default strict fallback (info), mode resolution failure (error), resolution race/reentry (warn), and attempted mode switch after startup immutability (error). In crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs, export_runtime_math_log_jsonl now emits runtime_mode_dispatch (trace, per decision), runtime_evidence_emitted (info), runtime_calibration with snapshot_capture_latency_ns + snapshot_validated_field_count (debug), runtime_snapshot (info), and runtime_snapshot_field_out_of_range (warn), all including trace/decision identifiers. Added/updated tests: mode_logging_captures_startup_selection_and_switch_attempt; runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields; snapshot_range_violations_identify_out_of_contract_fields. Verification (rch): cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets (pass), cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi mode_ -- --nocapture (pass), cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields -- --nocapture (pass), cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane snapshot_range_violations_identify_out_of_contract_fields -- --nocapture (pass). Remaining workspace fmt/clippy failures are pre-existing unrelated debt. Artifact refs: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs; crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs","created_at":"2026-02-25T06:09:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-obzgm","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] rpc_abi netname helpers walk unbounded user-supplied pointers (bd-z4k96 class)","description":"FOUND + FIXED via review round 5 (commit fe3f1c06).

Four POSIX RPC name-mapping entry points (host2netname, netname2host, netname2user, user2netname) dereferenced caller-supplied *const c_char via CStr::from_ptr with no length bound. A non-NUL-terminated host/domain/netname walked arbitrary process memory.

Reachability: every Sun-RPC client/server that uses Secure RPC name mapping (rpc.rusersd, NIS netname helpers, rpcbind authentication) hits these entry points.

Fix: read_bounded_cstr replacement; on None return 0.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commits 0c0b59f1 / 1d68447a / fe3f1c06; 3120/3120 abi tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bd-z4k96-class","high","review","unbounded-cstr"]}
 {"id":"bd-ofuo","title":"Conformance: execute supported unistd_ops fixture cases","description":"Add a direct frankenlibc_fixture_exec coverage test for the supported unistd_ops.json cases, expanding both-mode fixtures and documenting skips for symbolic fd cases that require pipe/open-file setup.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:24:59.747479429Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:29:58.612808167Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T06:29:58.610270356Z","close_reason":"Added direct supported unistd_ops fixture executor coverage and verified focused harness test/check/clippy through rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing","unistd"]}
+{"id":"bd-oh49d","title":"PORT: __libc_alloca_cutoff / __libc_use_alloca (glibc internal alloca-safety helpers)","description":"Port the glibc internal alloca-safety helpers: int __libc_alloca_cutoff(size_t size) and int __libc_use_alloca(size_t size). Both return non-zero iff `size` bytes can safely be allocated on the current threads stack via alloca() — glibc uses this to decide whether to fall back to malloc inside variable-length-array-style helpers. The threshold matches glibcs convention: min(PTHREAD_STACK_MIN/4, 65536). Some glibc-internal callers (NSS modules, locale parsers, sprintf width handling) link against these symbols when statically built. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers returning the bit-test result. Tests verify the cutoff value matches the expected threshold for typical sizes (small=1, large=0) and that __libc_use_alloca returns the same value as __libc_alloca_cutoff for any size.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:07:34.437948739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:07:34.437948739Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-olgi","title":"Track B: Implement dlinfo for RTLD_DI_LINKMAP and RTLD_DI_ORIGIN","description":"## Goal
Replace the dlinfo stub in glibc_internal_abi.rs (line 4290) that returns ENOSYS with a real implementation supporting the two most commonly used request codes.

## Current State
```rust
pub unsafe extern "C" fn dlinfo(_handle: *mut c_void, _request: c_int, _info: *mut c_void) -> c_int {
    unsafe { *libc::__errno_location() = libc::ENOSYS };
    -1
}
```

## Why This Matters
Programs using dlinfo include:
- Python (ctypes.util.find_library -> RTLD_DI_LINKMAP to walk loaded libs)
- Ruby FFI, JVM, Lua FFI for plugin/library discovery
- Debugging tools (ltrace, strace analysis scripts)
- Programs checking RTLD_DI_ORIGIN to find their own install directory

## Supported Request Codes

### RTLD_DI_LINKMAP (2) — Write struct link_map* to info
The struct link_map (defined in <link.h>) has this layout:
```c
struct link_map {
    ElfW(Addr) l_addr;    // Base address of shared object
    char      *l_name;    // Absolute file name of shared object
    ElfW(Dyn) *l_ld;      // Dynamic section pointer
    struct link_map *l_next, *l_prev;  // Chain of loaded objects
};
```

Implementation:
- For RTLD_DEFAULT / NULL handle: return a static link_map for libfrankenlibc_abi.so itself. Populate l_addr and l_name from /proc/self/maps by finding our own library. l_ld can be null. l_next/l_prev = null (single entry).
- For other handles: delegate to host dlinfo via resolve_host_symbol_raw('dlinfo') if available. This is the interpose model — host-loaded libraries are the host linker's responsibility.
- If host delegation unavailable: return -1 with errno=ENOENT.

### RTLD_DI_ORIGIN (6) — Write directory path string to info (char* buffer)
- Parse /proc/self/maps to find the pathname for the handle's load address range.
- Extract dirname (everything up to last '/').
- Copy into info buffer (caller must provide sufficient space — POSIX says PATH_MAX).
- For RTLD_DEFAULT: return dirname of libfrankenlibc_abi.so.
- For unknown handles: delegate to host or return -1 with EINVAL.

### Other request codes: Return -1 with errno=ENOSYS
RTLD_DI_SERINFO (4), RTLD_DI_SERINFOSIZE (5), RTLD_DI_TLS_MODID (9), RTLD_DI_TLS_DATA (10) — rare, remain ENOSYS.

## Design Details

### Self-identification
We already have /proc/self/maps parsing in host_resolve.rs (loaded_libc_base_and_path). Reuse that to find our own library path. Cache the result in a static OnceLock<(u64, String)>.

### Handle classification
- NULL or RTLD_DEFAULT (pseudo-handle): self
- Address within our .so's mapped range: self
- Otherwise: delegate to host dlinfo

### Static link_map for self
Allocate a static link_map struct (leaked Box) on first call. Populate with:
- l_addr = our base address from /proc/self/maps
- l_name = leaked CString of our library path
- l_ld = null (we don't expose our own dynamic section)
- l_next = l_prev = null

## Files Changed
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs: Replace dlinfo body (~60 lines)
- May add a helper fn in host_resolve.rs for self-identification

## Unit Tests (in tests/glibc_internal_abi_test.rs or tests/dlfcn_abi_test.rs)
- test dlinfo_origin_default_returns_valid_directory: dlinfo(RTLD_DEFAULT, RTLD_DI_ORIGIN, buf) writes a non-empty path that is a real directory (stat it)
- test dlinfo_origin_null_handle_returns_directory: dlinfo(NULL, RTLD_DI_ORIGIN, buf) same as above
- test dlinfo_linkmap_default_returns_nonnull: dlinfo(RTLD_DEFAULT, RTLD_DI_LINKMAP, &ptr) writes a non-null link_map* with valid l_name
- test dlinfo_linkmap_l_name_is_valid_path: the l_name in the returned link_map is a readable file path
- test dlinfo_unsupported_request_returns_enosys: dlinfo(NULL, 999, ptr) returns -1, errno=ENOSYS
- test dlinfo_bad_handle_returns_error: dlinfo(0xDEADBEEF, RTLD_DI_ORIGIN, buf) returns -1

## e2e Test Script: scripts/check_dlinfo_native.sh
Build and run a C test program under LD_PRELOAD that:
1. Calls dlinfo(RTLD_DEFAULT, RTLD_DI_ORIGIN, buf) and prints origin
2. Calls dlinfo(RTLD_DEFAULT, RTLD_DI_LINKMAP, &lm) and prints l_name
3. Verifies both return 0
4. Emits structured JSONL report

## Structured Logging
dlinfo emits: trace_id, api_family=dlfcn, symbol=dlinfo, request_code, handle_class (self|host|unknown), result, errno, latency_ns

## Acceptance Criteria
- dlinfo handles RTLD_DI_LINKMAP and RTLD_DI_ORIGIN for default/self handles
- Host delegation for non-self handles when host dlinfo available
- ENOSYS for unsupported request codes, EINVAL for bad handles
- 6+ unit tests pass
- e2e script passes under LD_PRELOAD
- cargo clippy clean
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite not regressed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:50.994367570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:48:34.754188981Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:48:34.753906196Z","close_reason":"Implemented dlinfo for RTLD_DI_LINKMAP and RTLD_DI_ORIGIN. Self-identification via /proc/self/maps (cached in OnceLock). RTLD_DI_LINKMAP: returns static leaked link_map with base addr and pathname for default/self handle, delegates to host for other handles. RTLD_DI_ORIGIN: extracts dirname from library path. Unsupported requests return ENOSYS. Clippy clean, LD_PRELOAD smoke passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dlfcn","native","stub-elimination"],"comments":[{"id":1362,"issue_id":"bd-olgi","author":"ubuntu","text":"dlinfo is a GNU extension (not POSIX). Key use cases: (1) RTLD_DI_LINKMAP - Python ctypes, Ruby FFI, JVM enumerate loaded libs. For interpose model, delegate to host dlinfo for host handles, return minimal struct for our own. (2) RTLD_DI_ORIGIN - directory of shared object from /proc/self/maps. RTLD_DI_SERINFO/SERINFOSIZE/TLS_MODID are rare, can remain ENOSYS.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-oraci","title":"dladdr returns 0 for valid function pointer","description":"execute_dladdr_case reports impl_output=0 (failure) for a valid function pointer where host glibc returns nonzero (success). Fixture loader_edges.json case dladdr_valid_address_strict expects 'nonzero' matching host. Real implementation gap in frankenlibc_abi::dlfcn_abi::dladdr (or the fixture executor's call shape). Exposed while adding harness-matrix coverage under bd-k2d8; the in-process + subprocess tests skip this specific case until the gap is fixed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:40:39.073201432Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:14:56.965372269Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:14:56.963016731Z","close_reason":"host_dladdr_cached now lazily bootstraps HOST_DLADDR via load_host_symbol; loader_edges_conformance_test::dladdr_valid_address_strict now passes without KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS skip. Committed 8cfba52 and pushed to main+master.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-os0u","title":"syscall: sched_getaffinity cpu_set veneer","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T09:25:58.430468203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T09:30:23.915577769Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T09:30:23.913032384Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
